subject: LU-12510 osd: osd-zfs to release zrlock quickly

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1:

Trevis Autotest2 started enforced review-ldiskfs session for build 66596. The following test parameters were specified: fortestonly=true.

Patch Set 1:

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs on CentOS 7.0/x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest2 from trevis-48vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/d2cda526-dc08-40a2-b206-f883d3363148 ran 4 tests.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

This makes sense as a quick workaround.  As long as the bonus dbuf is held, it will in turn keep a hold on the dnode meaning the zrlock can be dropped.

parents.subject: LU-9019 libcfs: avoid using HZ and msecs_to_jiffies()

commit.subject: LU-12510 osd: osd-zfs to release zrlock quickly
commit.message:
LU-12510 osd: osd-zfs to release zrlock quickly

otherwise few threads trying to access same dnode can get stuck.
this patch is a quick workaround for the issue, it's supposed
to be replaced with a better patch using regular DMU API.

Change-Id: I24d9ed7f8e68080c6a46409476a80799dbb45230
Signed-off-by: Alexey Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/35524
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Brian Behlendorf <behlendorf1@llnl.gov>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
